div1.entryFree:
Forfeiture, 1) that which is lost on engagement: by the exaction of the f. Merch. I, 3, 165. loose the f. IV, 1, 24. to cut the f. from that bankrupt there, IV, 1, 24 take thy f. IV, 1, 24 thou shalt have nothing but the f. IV, 1, 24
2) the falling due of a debt: the envious plea of f., of justice and his bond, Merch. III, 2, 285. I oft delivered from his --s many, III, 3, 22. will never grant this f. to hold, III, 3, 22 'twas due on f. Tim. II, 2, 30.
